comments.text: The trivial construction was with $S$ being set of powers of $2$ up to $N=2^{n-1}$. The best construction (by Bohman, ElJC'98) so far, has $N=(0.22002+o(1))\cdot 2^n$. So a construction with $N<2^n/5$ would be interesting, while not being a counterexample.
Yes. Here $\gg$ is the Vinogradov notation, which means 'up to an absolute constant'. In particular this problem cannot be disproved by any fixed finite example.
